item.title: Supreme Court sides with Monsanto in case over cancer risks from weedkiller
item.description: The Supreme Court ruled that Monsanto cannot be held liable under state laws for failing to warn consumers about the alleged cancer risks of its weedkiller Roundup on its label.

item.title: Cassidy says he passed a note to Witkoff during heated Trump meeting
item.description: Sen. Bill Cassidy said that after his exchange with the president, he passed a note to Steve Witkoff, saying he would consider changing his war powers vote.

item.title: NATO chief faces Trump's ire in meeting over Iran
item.description: President Trump criticized European allies for not supporting the U.S.-Israeli war with Iran during a meeting with NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte. CBS News' Aaron Navarro has more.

item.title: Supreme Court strikes down Hawaii law restricting guns on some private property
item.description: The Supreme Court struck down a Hawaii restriction that prohibits concealed-carry permit holders from bringing their firearms onto private property that is open to the public, like gas stations, restaurants or shops.

item.title: Are deadly heat waves driving Europeans to embrace air conditioning?
item.description: Only 20% European homes have AC, compared to 90% in the U.S., but as the climate changes, that vast gulf may be set to shrink.

item.title: Oil prices keep dropping as Oman rules out Strait of Hormuz "transit fees"
item.description: With the U.S.-Iran agreement appearing to hold, Oman rules out future Strait of Hormuz "transit fees" and oil prices continue their fall.

item.title: Prosecutors drop Harvey Weinstein's New York rape charge
item.description: New York prosecutors said they are dropping a rape charge against Harvey Weinstein instead of trying him for a fourth time.

item.title: Chris Evert announces her ovarian cancer has returned
item.description: American tennis legend Chris Evert announced that her ovarian cancer had returned in a social media post Thursday.

item.title: DeSantis confirms "Alligator Alcatraz" shutting down operations
item.description: Conditions at "Alligator Alcatraz" were criticized by lawyers, families and human rights groups who claimed detainees were mistreated.

item.title: How much will a $100,000 home equity loan cost monthly right now?
item.description: Home equity loan costs have dropped considerably. Here's what the monthly payment on a $100,000 loan will look like.

item.title: What happens to unpaid medical debt when someone dies?
item.description: Much of what you owe dies with you, but certain debts, like some medical bills, cling to those you leave behind.

item.title: Bill Cassidy on why he changed his war powers vote, heated Trump meeting
item.description: Sen. Bill Cassidy of Louisiana said he changed his support for a Democratic-led measure aimed at restricting President Trump's war powers in Iran after he secured a briefing from Middle East envoy Steve Witkoff by passing him a note during the heated meeting with the president. "Steve, I would consider changing my vote, but I've been voting yes because I've not been briefed," Cassidy recalled writing in the note in an interview with "Face the Nation" moderator Margaret Brennan that airs Sunday.

item.title: Taylor Swift wedding venue speculation fueled by MSG permit
item.description: There appear to be new clues about the location of Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce's wedding.

item.title: Stunning video shows plane's emergency crash landing in Alaska
item.description: Passenger Brett Fillipi was aboard a small commuter plane over Alaska when an emergency forced the plane's crash landing. Fillipi caught the dramatic footage on camera. Luckily, everyone on the plane survived. CBS News' Kris Van Cleave reports.

item.title: Venezuelan earthquake survivor says he "started to pray" as building shook
item.description: Buildings crumbled during back-to-back earthquakes in Venezuela, killing dozens. Search and rescue operations are underway, with the U.S. and other nations sending aid. CBS News' Lilia Luciano reports on conditions in Venezuela, and Virginia Tech geophysics professor Manoochehr Shirzaei comments on the science behind aftershocks.

item.title: Venezuela quake survivors describe devastation: "Everything collapsed"
item.description: A Caracas resident told CBS News that he "started to pray" when he felt the first earthquake hit Venezuela.

item.title: Supreme Court rules on two major immigration cases, siding with Trump
item.description: The Supreme Court released opinions on several major cases Thursday. In one case, justices sided with President Trump, allowing his administration to strip temporary protected status protections from Syrian and Haitian immigrants. CBS News' Camilo Montoya-Galvez and Jan Crawford report, plus legal contributor Rebecca Roiphe joins with more analysis.

item.title: Judge blocks Trump executive order on mail-in voting
item.description: President Trump signed an executive order in March requiring the creation of a list of U.S. citizens eligible to vote in each state and imposing stricter mail-in ballot rules.

item.title: Supreme Court lets Trump strip deportation protections from Syrians, Haitians
item.description: The Supreme Court on Thursday said the Trump administration can move forward with its efforts to strip more than 356,000 Syrian and Haitian immigrants of temporary protections.

item.title: "Excuse My Grandma" hosts tell how podcast has influence their relationship and perspectives
item.description: Kim Murstein and her grandma Gail Gudnick, the hosts of the popular podcast "Excuse My Grandma," tell "CBS Mornings" how the pandemic helped launch their podcast, its impact on their relationship and what they've learned from each other.

item.title: White nationalist groups are now recruiting young women
item.description: United Youth, a white nationalist organization that oversees groups for young men across the country, now has the first known women's group, Young Columbia.

item.title: Big Tech is all in on AI. Now all they need is customers.
item.description: Technology companies are betting trillions of dollars that consumers will open their wallets for AI services. But what if Big Tech is wrong?

item.title: Poland's deputy prime minister warns of possible Russian "false flag operation"
item.description: Poland's deputy prime minister tells CBS News he "wouldn't exclude the Russians doing some kind of false flag operation" to justify an attack on NATO.

item.title: California officials use license plate readers, drones to crack down on porch pirates
item.description: To stop porch pirates, Irvine police have been using aerial drones to follow suspects from residential areas and shopping malls.

item.title: Polish Deputy Prime Minister Radek Sikorski warns Russia could launch a "false flag operation"
item.description: Polish Deputy Prime Minister Radek Sikorski said Russia could launch a "false flag operation" to try and justify an attack on a U.S. ally. "I wouldn't exclude the Russians doing some kind of false flag operation against Russian territory in order to have a pretext for hitting one of the NATO countries," Sikorski told CBS News' Aidan Stretch.

item.title: Fed's preferred inflation gauge hits 3-year high
item.description: Inflation continued to rise in May, with the Personal Consumption Expenditures price index rising at an annual rate of 4.1%.

item.title: Astronomers find 2 gigantic planets lighter than cotton candy
item.description: The featherweight pair — orbiting a star 1,110 light-years away — are the biggest exoplanets found to have less density than cotton candy.

item.title: IBM unveils tech for higher-performance chips that use much less power
item.description: IBM has raised the curtain on semiconductor technology it says could deliver computer chips with 50 percent better performance while dramatically lowering power consumption.

item.title: Democrats to propose bill capping out-of-pocket Medicare costs for enrollees
item.description: Some Senate Democrats want to cap the amount beneficiaries in traditional Medicare have to pay toward care, but the move is expected to draw GOP opposition for potentially adding billions to Medicare costs.

item.title: Company owned by Trump donor won $1.7 million no-bid Reflecting Pool contract
item.description: The federal government awarded a company owned by a Trump donor $1.7 million to install a new water cleaning system for the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool, records show.
